feature_name: Dual operation modes: Root Complex (RC) and End Point (EP)
description: The PCIe core is dual-mode and can operate as either a Root Complex or an End Point. Mode is selected via bootstrap pins PCIESSMODE[1:0] and can be overridden in software. In RC mode the module enumerates and manages downstream devices; in EP mode it exposes BARs and responds to configuration. The application registers and many capabilities are accessible in both modes. Software must configure mode before enabling link training.
evidence: Section 1.4.1 PCI Express Core Module; CMD_STATUS.LTSSM_EN
dependencies: Device bootstrap PCIESSMODE[1:0]; DEVSTAT; CMD_STATUS
Remarks: Select mode early in boot; avoid changing after link up.

feature_name: Link width and speed: x1/x2, Gen1/Gen2 (2.5/5.0 GT/s)
description: The controller supports a single bidirectional link with up to two lanes (×2). Raw line rates of 2.5 or 5.0 Gbps per lane are supported, with 8b/10b encoding overhead. Gen2 enable is controlled and negotiated after Gen1 bring-up. De-emphasis and swing settings are provided for Gen2 signaling. Lane enable and negotiated width are software-readable.
evidence: Features (1.3); LINK_CAP.MAX_LINK_WIDTH/MAX_LINK_SPEED; LINK_STAT_CTRL.LINK_SPEED; PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD, DEEMPH, CFG_TX_SWING
dependencies: PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD=1 to request Gen2; LNK_MODE/LN_EN set for lane count; proper SerDes settings
Remarks: Ensure common clock and proper equalization when targeting Gen2 ×2.

feature_name: VBUSM interfaces: 128-bit master and slave ports
description: The PCIe block exposes one 128-bit VBUSM master port and one 128-bit VBUSM slave port to the SoC interconnect. The master port services inbound PCIe requests into internal memory. The slave port is used for application MMR access and for outbound data transfers. Proper interconnect bandwidth is required to sustain PCIe throughput in both directions.
dependencies: SoC interconnect clocks and QoS; PRIORITY.MST_PRIORITY
Remarks: Provision interconnect bandwidth (~85% of 2/4 Gbps per lane per direction).

feature_name: Outbound address translation (32 regions, 1–8 MB each)
description: Outbound TLPs map internal addresses to PCIe addresses via 32 equal-sized translation regions. Region size is set in OB_SIZE and offsets in OB_OFFSET_INDEXn/OB_OFFSETn_HI. The index is derived from internal address bits based on region size. Only memory read/write transactions use outbound translation; large transactions must not cross translation boundaries.
dependencies: CMD_STATUS.OB_XLT_EN=1; OB_SIZE/OB_OFFSET* programmed
Remarks: Choose region sizes to align with buffers; avoid boundary crossings per 2.7.1.1.

feature_name: Inbound address translation (4 regions) with BAR matching
description: Inbound requests are remapped to internal addresses using up to four regions. Each region associates to a BAR via IB_BARn and defines start and offset via IB_STARTn_LO/HI and IB_OFFSETn. Only BAR1–BAR5 map to Address Space 1; BAR0 maps to application/config space. Translation computes offset = TLP address − start, then adds the programmed offset to form internal address.
dependencies: CMD_STATUS.IB_XLT_EN=1; BARs configured; IB_* programmed
Remarks: Plan non-overlapping windows; ensure transactions do not cross 4 KB boundaries per PCIe spec.

feature_name: BAR0 inbound exception to application registers
description: Inbound accesses that hit BAR0 are always routed to the PCIESS application registers (Address Space 0). This behavior is fixed in both RC and EP modes and cannot be disabled. It enables remote configuration and messaging to work consistently. Software must account for this when designing inbound mappings.
dependencies: BAR0 configured; Address Space 0 mapping
Remarks: Never attempt to remap BAR0 to data buffers; reserve for control.

description: For BAR1, an inbound region can use the BAR1 value as the start address when IB_START is left zero. When enabled, only one inbound translation window relative to BAR1 is available. No other BARs or inbound windows can be used concurrently with this special mode. This can simplify EP designs needing a single contiguous window.
evidence: Section 2.7.2.3 Using BAR1 Value As Start Address
dependencies: IB_BARn=1; IB_STARTn_LO/HI=0; IB_OFFSETn set
Remarks: Use only when a single inbound window suffices; otherwise configure explicit starts.

description: The device supports Type 0 and Type 1 headers, and BARs are writable via internal bus prior to enumeration. BAR Mask registers overlay the BARs when CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2 is set, controlling requested size/type and enable. BAR0 mask is limited to bits [31:20]; BAR mask writes are write-only and must be verified indirectly. Software must clear DBI_CS2 before enumeration.
evidence: Sections 2.7.3 and 3.3/3.4; CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2; BARx and BARx_MASK regs
dependencies: Enable CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2 to write masks; then clear; verify via test patterns
Remarks: Do not modify BAR masks from the serial link side; may lead to undefined behavior.

feature_name: Address Space 0 layout (regs, local/remote config, IO window)
description: Address Space 0 provides a 16 KB window partitioned into four 4 KB regions. These are: application registers, local PCIe configuration (Type 0/1), remote configuration access, and an RC-only 4 KB IO access window. These regions must be accessed as 32-bit non-burst, non-cacheable transactions. Software should avoid access when the link is down.
dependencies: Proper mapping of BAR0; internal bus access rules
Remarks: Add link-up checks before remote config/IO window access to prevent hangs.

feature_name: Address Space 1 mapping to data windows
description: Address Space 1 is used for data transfers and may map to multiple endpoints in RC mode. BAR values define where an EP’s memory appears in the CPU memory map. Outbound and inbound translators convert between local and PCIe address spaces as configured. The space is large and not necessarily contiguous, requiring careful software region management.
dependencies: BAR configuration and translation tables
Remarks: Create a software map of device windows to avoid overlaps and to enforce access sizes.

feature_name: No built-in DMA engine; EDMA/SoC DMA usage
description: The PCIESS has no internal DMA engine. In RC mode, a system DMA (e.g., EDMA) can source/sink bursts via the slave port for outbound transactions. In EP mode, inbound PCIe writes land on the master port without DMA, while outbound reads/writes can be initiated by software or DMA through the slave port. TI provides EDMA examples for memory write/read over PCIe.
dependencies: External EDMA; interconnect bandwidth; OB/IB translation enabled
Remarks: Size bursts to max payload (128 B) and align to improve efficiency.

feature_name: Interrupts: legacy INTx and MSI support (EP/RC)
description: The module supports generating legacy INTx in EP mode and receiving them in RC mode. MSI is supported for both generation and reception, with up to 32 vectors. MSI address/data are configured via MSI capability registers; writes to MSI_IRQ register generate MSI events. Interrupt events are mapped to 14 module interrupts, with enable/status/EOI control.
dependencies: Enable MSIn_IRQ_ENABLE_SET; configure MSI_CAP.MSI_EN; INTx disabled when MSI enabled
Remarks: Clear status via MSIn_IRQ_STATUS and write IRQ_EOI to avoid retrigger.

feature_name: MSI generation to RC and peer EP
description: MSI is generated by issuing a 32-bit memory write to the configured MSI address/data. The module also allows an EP to target another EP’s BAR0 MSI_IRQ to signal peer interrupts, though this is outside base PCIe spec. Proper vector mapping and address programming are required. Writes may be dropped if status is not cleared before retrigger.
dependencies: MSI_CAP configured; destination BAR0/RC MSI_IRQ reachable; vector within 0–31
Remarks: Document vector ownership per device; ensure ordering if relaxed ordering is used.

description: The device implements standard PCIe device power states. D0 is the active state, with D0 initialized after enumeration and D0 uninitialized after reset. D1 and D2 are optional light-sleep states driven by software policy. D3hot retains power for config access; D3cold removes power and requires re-initialization. PME capabilities and reporting are configurable.
dependencies: PM_CTL_STAT.PWR_STATE; PMCAP fields; PME enable
Remarks: Quiesce outstanding TLPs before transitions; honor PME and timing requirements.

description: Active State Power Management supports autonomous transitions to L0s and L1. Register control enables L0s-only or L0s+L1 entry, and common-clock affects latencies. Software can also drive L1 upon entering D1/D2/D3hot. Additional tuning via ACK_FREQ allows adjusting entry latencies and NFTS for L0s/L0 transitions.
dependencies: LINK_CAP.AS_LINK_PM; LINK_STAT_CTRL.ACTIVE_LINK_PM; COMMON_CLK_CFG
Remarks: Verify link partner capabilities; choose latency classes consistent with system timing.

description: The module supports hardware Power-On Reset, System Reset (hard/soft), and software-initiated hot reset. Software can assert INIT_RST to issue downstream hot reset and must wait at least 100 ms before transactions post-reset. Sticky bits in MMRs may retain across soft reset; power-domain cycling resets the module fully.
dependencies: PSC power domain control; RSTCMD; observe timing
Remarks: Ensure traffic is quiesced before power down; re-run initialization sequence after reset.

description: RC bring-up requires enabling SerDes PLL, powering the module, selecting RC mode, disabling LTSSM, programming configuration, then enabling LTSSM. Link-up is confirmed when DEBUG0.LTSSM_STATE reports 0x11 (L0). After link, perform enumeration, BAR setup, and downstream initialization. Timeouts and ordering must be handled in software.
dependencies: SerDes PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL; PCIE_SERDES_STS.LOCK; CMD_STATUS
Remarks: Set COMMON_CLK_CFG appropriately; use CFG_SETUP for Type0/1 config cycles.

description: EP bring-up mirrors RC steps with PCIESSMODE set to EP. After PLL lock and configuration, assert LTSSM_EN and wait for L0. During post-link configuration, APP_RETRY_EN can be set to force Retry responses until the EP is ready, then cleared to accept transactions. BARs and inbound translations must be defined before enabling bus mastering.
dependencies: PCIESSMODE EP; BARx and IB_* programmed; STATUS_COMMAND.BUS_MS set by RC
Remarks: Coordinate with RC for MSI/INTx choice and BAR assignment.

description: The controller supports PIPE layer loopback for compliance and diagnostics. As loopback master, software sets PL_LINK_CTRL.LPBK_EN and retrains (RC) or forces recovery (EP) to enter loopback. As loopback slave, no programming is required; test equipment can drive entry. PIPE loopback is symbol-level and does not return TLPs.
dependencies: Proper LTSSM control (PL_FORCE_LINK for EP); link partner or tester
Remarks: Use for PHY/Link compliance only; not for end-to-end transaction testing.

description: PHY loopback internally routes TX to RX, enabling TLP loopback without a partner. Configure SERDES_CFGx to enable TX/RX loopback and disable RX_LOS, then start link training. Force LTSSM to POLL_ACTIVE via PL_FORCE_LINK to finalize. Bar and Base/Limit settings must avoid misrouting during loopback; configuration TLPs are not usable.
dependencies: SerDes loopback bits; PL_FORCE_LINK; CMD_STATUS.LTSSM_EN
Remarks: Configure before any traffic to avoid sequence number mismatches; avoid config-type TLPs.

description: Although the PCIe core is little-endian on the wire, the subsystem supports big-endian data presentation. ENDIAN[1:0] selects swap granularity (1/2/4/8-byte) for inbound and outbound paths. Tables specify byte-lane mappings for both directions. MMR and non-prefetchable accesses are always handled on 32-bit boundaries.
dependencies: ENDIAN register set when system endianness is big endian
Remarks: Verify FBE/LBE settings on TLPs; keep MMR accesses 32-bit aligned.

description: Maximum outbound payload is 128 bytes; inbound up to 256 bytes, with max remote read of 256 bytes. Outbound bus transactions must be ≤128 bytes and aligned; if misaligned, limit to 120 bytes. Only incremental bursts are supported; fixed/wrap bursts are unsupported and can cause lockups. Byte enables must form a single contiguous span in writes.
dependencies: DMA engine settings; interconnect burst types; address alignment
Remarks: Split large buffers accordingly and enforce 128-byte boundaries to avoid splits.

description: The device supports PCI-compatible error bits, PCIe baseline error reporting, and Advanced Error Reporting. Software enables reporting via DEV_STAT_CTRL and ROOT_CTRL_CAP, and masks/severity via PCIE_UNCERR_MASK/CERR_MASK and PCIE_UNCERR_SVRTY. Root status collects fatal/non-fatal/correctable message indications. Error header logs capture failing TLP header DW0–DW3.
dependencies: Enable *_RP bits; configure masks/severity; handle interrupts (ERR IRQs)
Remarks: Classify and log first error pointer; clear W1C bits to re-arm reporting.

description: End-to-end CRC generation and checking are supported and controllable. PCIE_ACCR.ECRC_GEN_EN and ECRC_CHK_EN enable generation and checking respectively, with capability bits advertising support. A first-error pointer helps diagnose the earliest uncorrectable error. ECRC should be disabled if link partners do not support it, especially when acting as RC.
dependencies: PCIE_ACCR ECRC_GEN_EN/ECRC_CHK_EN settings
Remarks: Coordinate capability with link partner; avoid false errors when peers lack ECRC.

feature_name: Debug and link state visibility (DEBUG0/DEBUG1, LTSSM)
description: DEBUG0 and DEBUG1 expose PIPE data, skip events, LTSSM state, and training status. LTSSM state encodings are provided for state machine diagnostics. Additional port logic registers provide link status (DLL_ACTIVE, COMMON_CLK_CFG) and negotiated width/speed. These are critical for bring-up and field troubleshooting.
evidence: Sections 3.9.11/3.9.12; Appendix A LTSSM States; LINK_STAT_CTRL
dependencies: Readable DEBUG0/DEBUG1; ensure clocks present; LTSSM enabled
Remarks: Poll during bring-up to sequence configuration and detect training regressions.

feature_name: Port Logic controls: force link, speed change, de-emphasis
description: Software can direct link behavior via Port Logic and Link Control 2 registers. PL_FORCE_LINK can force LTSSM states for test/recovery. LINK_CTRL2 selects target link speed, compliance entry, and de-emphasis. PL_GEN2 controls de-emphasis, TX swing, and requests a directed speed change after Gen1 link-up. These controls aid interoperability and tuning.
dependencies: Proper sequencing with LTSSM and partner capabilities
Remarks: Use only when needed; some fields are sticky across hot reset and must be cleared.

description: The PHY uses a differential reference clock and internal PLL to generate functional clocks. Many systems use a common REFCLK and SSC; COMMON_CLK_CFG in LINK_STAT_CTRL must reflect the architecture. The PCIESS registers are only accessible once the SerDes PLL is locked. Pipe common-clock usage affects link training sequences and latencies.
dependencies: Stable 100 MHz refclk; PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL.ENPLL=1; lock check
Remarks: Increase training sequences if no common clock; configure SSC environments carefully.

description: KeyStone I devices expose chip-level SerDes registers to configure PLL, loop bandwidth, and reference clock multiplication. ENPLL starts the PLL and LOCK indicates status. MPY is chosen to achieve 2.5 GHz PLL output for both Gen1 and Gen2, with data bus width conversion for Gen2. Proper LB and VRANGE settings optimize jitter tolerance.
dependencies: PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL.ENPLL=1; MPY per refclk; wait for PCIE_SERDES_STS.LOCK
Remarks: Allow 1 µs for regulator stabilization before lock polling; select LB per clock quality.

feature_name: Physical pins and lanes (no pin mux)
description: The module exposes differential pairs for up to two lanes (TX/RX per lane) and differential refclock inputs. Functional PCIe pins are not multiplexed with other signals; only bootstrap pins for mode/power are latched via GPIO/Timer. Proper power, ground, and terminations are required for PHY bring-up.
dependencies: Board-level refclk and SerDes supplies; termination networks
Remarks: Follow TI SerDes layout and power guidelines to meet signal integrity.

description: The design does not support splitting x2 into two x1 links, multiple virtual channels, or multiple traffic classes. There is no function-level reset, in-band beacon for wake, built-in hot plug, inbound IO access, or non-incremental burst addressing. No auxiliary power retention for D3cold and no support for L2 link state are provided. Software must design around these limitations.
dependencies: System architecture must avoid these requirements
Remarks: Use external switches for multi-port; avoid wrap/fixed bursts and inbound IO.
